messages.content: Build a pure Decider for an AirCrew aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve using discriminated unions. Reject rostering a crew member onto a departed flight.
Build a Turnaround Decider that tracks ground servicing between arrival and next departure. Commands: beginTurnaround, completePhase, closeTurnaround. Reject closing when not all phases are done.
Build a GateAssignment Decider that assigns an arriving aircraft to a gate and releases it. Commands: requestGate, assignGate, releaseGate. Reject assigning a gate already occupied.

Write a pure Decider for a ServicingCheck aggregate (pre-flight maintenance check). Commands schedule the check, record each inspection item, and sign off. Reject sign-off when any item is still open.
Build a pure Decider for AirlineItinerary that tracks a multi-leg journey. Commands: bookItinerary, addLeg, confirm. Reject confirmation when itinerary has zero legs.

Write a flat read-model projection for AirCrew stream. Project AirCrewCreated, CrewMemberAssigned, and AirCrewDeparted into a denormalised view keyed by crew id showing member count and departure time.
What's the v5 TypeScript way to build a read-model projection for GateAssignment? Show a minimal example with a denormalised gate occupancy view.
Project Turnaround events into a status-board view. Only include active turnarounds with pending phases. Use a pure project(view, event) function.
Build an AirportSlot utilisation projection that counts slots in use per airport. Skip any events unrelated to slots. Must use project(view, event).
Explain briefly how a Snapshot with versioned upgrade works, then show a typed implementation for an AirCrew state snapshot that migrates v1 -> v2 -> v3.
Build a versioned Snapshot loader for ServicingCheck that rehydrates from older shapes. Current version 3. Include v1->v2 and v2->v3 upgraders with typed assertions.
Write a TypeScript Snapshot helper for AirlineItinerary with a CURRENT_VERSION of 2. Include a v1->v2 upgrader that adds a cancellationReason field.
How do I implement optimistic concurrency with expectedRevision for a GateAssignment stream? Show a local EventStore interface and a write path that converts store conflicts into ConcurrencyConflict.
Write an appendWithExpectedRevision helper for Turnaround streams. Translate the store's mismatch error into a typed ConcurrencyConflict. Use a local EventStore interface.
Write a TypeScript Effect.gen wrapper around an EventStoreDB-style client that does an optimistic-concurrency append for TailNumber. Surface ConcurrencyConflict as a typed error channel.
Build a CommandHandler over an EventStore for AirCrew. Load the stream, call decide, then append with expectedRevision. Include the Decider trio wired to a local EventStore interface.
Explain briefly how a CommandHandler over an EventStore orchestrates, then show a typed implementation for Turnaround.
Write a CommandHandler for GateAssignment. Constraints: strict types, no any, wrap the IO boundary with Effect.gen and surface errors as typed values.
Build a CommandHandler for AirlineItinerary that uses appendToStream and readStream from a local EventStore interface. Commands: bookItinerary, addLeg.
Write a generic aggregateStream<S,E> helper that replays a stream into state. Use for await and a typed evolve parameter. No mutation of the input.
Implement aggregateStream for a FuelUplift stream and a when folder. Walk the stream with for await and return the folded state.
Explain briefly how when(state,event) differs from project(view,event), then show aggregateStream replaying PilotLog events.
Write a rebuild function for the AirportSlot read model. Read batches from revision 0, project each event, persist the batch, and advance the cursor. Support resuming from a passed-in revision.
Rebuild the BaggageClaim airline read model from revision 0 in batches, with progress callbacks. Support crash-resume via a from: bigint option.
Build a CQRS boundary for GateAssignment. handleCommand writes through a Decider and EventStore; queryView reads from a separate read store. Share the event DU but not state.

Refactor this inline v5 handler to split CQRS read/write properly, with queryView using its own eventually-consistent store: ```typescript async function handleOrRead(cmd: {type:'book';id:string;pax:string}|{type:'q';id:string}, store:{ readStream:(n:string)=>Promise<{revision:bigint;data:{type:'ItineraryBooked';id:string;pax:string}}[]>; appendToStream:(n:string,e:unknown[],o:{expectedRevision:bigint|'no_stream'|'any'})=>Promise<void> }){ if(cmd.type==='q'){ const r = await store.readStream('itin-'+cmd.id); return r.map(x=>x.data); } const r = await store.readStream('itin-'+cmd.id); await store.appendToStream('itin-'+cmd.id,[{type:'ItineraryBooked',id:cmd.id,pax:cmd.pax}],{expectedRevision: r.length===0?'no_stream':r[r.length-1].revision}); }```

Write an outbox pattern helper for AirCrew: in one transactional unit, insert outbox rows and append events to the stream. Leave a commentary comment describing how the publisher worker polls and marks rows published.

Fix this v4-style handler to typed ES v5 idioms with proper exhaustiveness and a local EventStore interface: ```typescript function handleCmd(cmd, events){ if(cmd.type==='register'){ return [{type:'TailRegistered', tail:cmd.tail}]; } if(cmd.type==='retire'){ return [{type:'TailRetired'}]; } }```

Targeted fix: this decide throws a string on invalid command — convert it to a typed DomainError class and add exhaustive never: ```typescript function decide(c: {type:'schedule'|'close'}, s: {status:'idle'|'active'}){ if(c.type==='schedule' && s.status!=='idle') throw 'BAD'; if(c.type==='close' && s.status!=='active') throw 'BAD'; }```
Write a typed projection for PilotLog that rolls up total flight hours per pilot. Include an emptyView and a pure project function.
Write an Effect.gen wrapper over an EventStore that loads a TailNumber stream with readFromStream and folds it into state. Expose errors in the typed error channel.
